index 3a1b2c4..5d6e7f8 100644
--- a/src/app/core/auth.py
+++ b/src/app/core/auth.py
@@ -1,15 +1,45 @@
-from datetime import datetime
-from typing import Optional
+from datetime import datetime, timedelta
+from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
+from enum import Enum
 
-class TokenService:
-    def create_token(self, user_id: int) -> str:
-        pass
+class TokenType(Enum):
+    ACCESS = "access"
+    REFRESH = "refresh"
+
+class TokenClaims:
+    def __init__(self, user_id: int, token_type: TokenType, ttl_seconds: int):
+        self.user_id = user_id
+        self.token_type = token_type
+        self.issued_at = datetime.utcnow()
+        self.expires_at = self.issued_at + timedelta(seconds=ttl_seconds)
+        self.jti = self._generate_jti()
+
+    def _generate_jti(self) -> str:
+        import uuid
+        return str(uuid.uuid4())
+
+    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        return {
+            "sub": str(self.user_id),
+            "type": self.token_type.value,
+            "iat": int(self.issued_at.timestamp()),
+            "exp": int(self.expires_at.timestamp()),
+            "jti": self.jti,
+        }
+
+class TokenService:
+    ACCESS_TTL = 900
+    REFRESH_TTL = 86400 * 7
+
+    def create_token(self, user_id: int, token_type: TokenType = TokenType.ACCESS) -> str:
+        ttl = self.ACCESS_TTL if token_type == TokenType.ACCESS else self.REFRESH_TTL
+        claims = TokenClaims(user_id=user_id, token_type=token_type, ttl_seconds=ttl)
+        return self._encode(claims.to_dict())
+
+    def _encode(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
+        import jwt
+        secret = self._get_secret()
+        return jwt.encode(payload, secret, algorithm="HS256")
+
+    def _get_secret(self) -> str:
+        import os
+        secret = os.environ.get("JWT_SECRET")
+        if not secret:
+            raise RuntimeError("JWT_SECRET environment variable not set")
+        return secret
 
diff --git a/src/app/core/auth.py b/src/app/core/auth.py
@@ -45,0 +46,30 @@
+    def validate_token(self, token: str) -> Optional[TokenClaims]:
+        try:
+            payload = self._decode(token)
+            return self._claims_from_payload(payload)
+        except Exception:
+            return None
+
+    def _decode(self, token: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+        import jwt
+        secret = self._get_secret()
+        return jwt.decode(token, secret, algorithms=["HS256"])
+
+    def _claims_from_payload(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> TokenClaims:
+        user_id = int(payload["sub"])
+        token_type = TokenType(payload["type"])
+        ttl = self.ACCESS_TTL if token_type == TokenType.ACCESS else self.REFRESH_TTL
+        claims = TokenClaims(user_id=user_id, token_type=token_type, ttl_seconds=ttl)
+        claims.jti = payload["jti"]
+        return claims
+
+    def is_expired(self, token: str) -> bool:
+        try:
+            payload = self._decode(token)
+            exp = payload.get("exp", 0)
+            return datetime.utcnow().timestamp() > exp
+        except Exception:
+            return True
+
+    def revoke_token(self, jti: str) -> None:
+        self._revocation_store.add(jti)
+

+import pytest
+from app.core.auth import TokenService, TokenType
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def token_service():
+    return TokenService()
+
+
+class TestTokenCreation:
+    def test_access_token_created(self, token_service, monkeypatch):
+        monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", "test-secret")
+        token = token_service.create_token(user_id=1)
+        assert token is not None
+        assert isinstance(token, str)
+
+    def test_refresh_token_created(self, token_service, monkeypatch):
+        monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", "test-secret")
+        token = token_service.create_token(user_id=1, token_type=TokenType.REFRESH)
+        assert token is not None
+
+    def test_token_contains_user_id(self, token_service, monkeypatch):
+        monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", "test-secret")
+        token = token_service.create_token(user_id=42)
+        claims = token_service.validate_token(token)
+        assert claims is not None
+        assert claims.user_id == 42
+
+    def test_token_type_in_claims(self, token_service, monkeypatch):
+        monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", "test-secret")
+        token = token_service.create_token(user_id=1, token_type=TokenType.REFRESH)
+        claims = token_service.validate_token(token)
+        assert claims.token_type == TokenType.REFRESH
+
+    def test_raises_without_secret(self, token_service):
+        import os
+        os.environ.pop("JWT_SECRET", None)
+        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="JWT_SECRET"):
+            token_service.create_token(user_id=1)
+
+class TestTokenValidation:
+    def test_valid_token_returns_claims(self, token_service, monkeypatch):
+        monkeypatch.setenv("JWT_SECRET", "test-secret")
+        token = token_service.create_token(user_id=7)
+        result = token_service.validate_token(token)
+        assert result is not None

system_prompt_baseline:
You are a senior ML engineer. Tech stack: Python + FastAPI + pytest + Docker + Hexagonal Architecture.

Rules:
- TDD: always write tests first, never adapt tests to code
- Hexagonal arch: no framework imports in core/
- Commits: subject line only, ≤72 chars, NO Co-Authored-By footers
- Plan mode required for multi-file changes
- Never hardcode secrets

## Project Context: TechCon Defectoscopy Platform

This is an industrial defect detection system for welded joints in pipeline infrastructure. The system processes ultrasonic testing (UT) data from automated scanning rigs and produces real-time defect classifications for field engineers.

### Business Requirements
The platform must process scan data within 3 seconds of acquisition to enable real-time quality control on the production line. False negative rate (missed defects) must be under 0.1% as per GOST R 55724-2013 standards. False positive rate should remain below 5% to avoid excessive production halts. The system integrates with existing SAP ERP for work order management and defect reporting.

### Current Architecture
The system follows hexagonal architecture with these layers:
- api/: FastAPI routers for scan ingestion, result retrieval, and operator dashboard
- core/: Pure domain logic — DefectClassifier, ScanProcessor, ReportGenerator
- services/: Application layer orchestrating ML inference, notification, and audit
- adapters/: PostgreSQL (scan storage), Redis (result cache), S3 (scan archives), MLflow (model registry), GPU worker RPC
- models/: Pydantic schemas for ScanJob, DefectReport, OperatorAlert

### ML Model Details
Current production model: YOLOv8-based defect detector fine-tuned on 45,000 labeled UT scan images. Model version: v3.2.1 (deployed 2026-03-15). Input: 512x512 grayscale scan slices. Output: defect bounding boxes with class labels (porosity, crack, inclusion, delamination) and confidence scores. Inference runs on NVIDIA A100 GPU via dedicated worker service. Model artifacts stored in YC Object Storage under s3://techcon-models/defectoscopy/.

### Infrastructure
Production: 3-node K8s cluster on Yandex Cloud (2x CPU workers + 1x GPU worker). Staging: single VM yc-dev-stand-01 (89.169.178.111) with docker-compose. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with build/test/push/deploy stages. Container registry: cr.yandex/crp.../techcon-defectoscopy. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus + custom alerting via Telegram bot.

### Known Technical Debt
1. ScanProcessor.process_batch() is synchronous — blocks the event loop for large batches (>100 slices). Needs asyncification with background task queue.
2. DefectClassifier has direct import of torch — violates hexagonal arch rule (no framework imports in core/). Needs interface extraction.
3. Report generation uses string formatting instead of templating engine — fragile and hard to maintain.
4. No retry logic in GPU worker RPC calls — transient GPU worker restarts cause 500 errors.
5. Alembic migration 0015 has no downgrade() — violates migration safety rules.

### Team Conventions
- Feature branches: feature/DEFECTO-NNN-short-description
- Hotfixes: hotfix/DEFECTO-NNN-description
- All PRs require 2 approvals + CI green
- Release tags: vX.Y.Z-defectoscopy
- Sprint duration: 2 weeks, current sprint: DEFECTO-S24 (ends 2026-04-18)
- Backlog managed in YouTrack project DEFECTO

messages_baseline.content:
Hexagonal Architecture (also known as Ports and Adapters, coined by Alistair Cockburn) is a software design pattern that isolates the core application logic from external concerns.

The key idea: your domain logic lives at the center and knows nothing about databases, HTTP frameworks, or external APIs. All external dependencies connect through well-defined interfaces (ports), and the concrete implementations of those interfaces are the adapters.

In practice for a FastAPI + Python project:
- core/ contains pure Python classes with no imports from FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, or any external library
- adapters/ contains the SQLAlchemy repositories, HTTP clients, S3 wrappers
- api/ contains FastAPI routers that translate HTTP requests into domain calls
- services/ orchestrates: receives input from api/, calls core/ logic, delegates I/O to adapters/

The test benefit is significant: you can test all core/ logic without a database or HTTP server, injecting mock adapters through constructor injection.
